title: Count Subarrays With Majority Element I
content:
You are given an integer array nums and an integer target.

Return the number of subarrays of nums in which target is the majority element.

The majority element of a subarray is the element that appears strictly more than half of the times in that subarray.

Example 1:

Input: nums = [1,2,2,3], target = 2

Output: 5

Explanation: Valid subarrays with target = 2 as the majority element:

• nums[1..1] = [2]

• nums[2..2] = [2]

• nums[1..2] = [2,2]

• nums[0..2] = [1,2,2]

• nums[1..3] = [2,2,3]

So there are 5 such subarrays.

Example 2:

Input: nums = [1,1,1,1], target = 1

Output: 10

Explanation: ​​​​​​​All 10 subarrays have 1 as the majority element.

Example 3:

Input: nums = [1,2,3], target = 4

Output: 0

Explanation: target = 4 does not appear in nums at all. Therefore, there cannot be any subarray where 4 is the majority element. Hence the answer is 0.

Constraints:

• 1 <= nums.length <= 1000

• 1 <= nums[i] <= 10​​​​​​​9

• 1 <= target <= 109

class Solution(object):
    def countMajoritySubarrays(self, nums, target):

        :type nums: List[int]
        :type target: int
        :rtype: int
        """

hints: Count all subarrays where 2 * count(target) > length

solutions.explanation:
Brute force O(n²): for each starting index i, scan right counting target occurrences. Majority when 2*count > length.

Time: O(n²). Space: O(1).
